stemHtml: Which of the following is a complete and correct definition of low-level windshear?

options.a: Windshear, microbursts, turbulence, wake vortices, and virga encountered in any phase of flight.
options.b: Windshear associated with jetstreams in mid-latitudes or rotors associated with buildings, obstacles, and/or topography.
options.d: Windshear along the final approach path or along the runway, and along the take-off and initial climb-out flight paths.
